title: dianthus spurius
intro: Dianthus spurius, also known as the fringe pink or pink grass pink, is a species of flowering plant in the carnation family (Caryophyllaceae). The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to soothe wounds and reduce swelling. In herbal preparations, the flowers of Dianthus spurius are often used to make teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are believed to offer various health benefits, including reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs. Additionally, the plant's roots and leaves are sometimes used in herbal medicine to treat digestive issues and promote overall well-being.
